details.body: Wire planned-elsewhere findings through the actual final-review/reconciliation path instead of only adding a standalone helper. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:137-154` still only allows free-form amendments, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1523-1553` still marks any accepted amendment as `restart_required`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7359-7378` still turns every accepted final-review item into a `QueuedAmendment`. The new handler in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:81-158` is not invoked from that flow, so there is currently no way to carry `mapped_to_bead_id` through review or let the active bead complete without a reopen/restart loop.

Make `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` transactional across `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` and the milestone journal. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2352-2380` the mapping file is appended first and the `PlannedElsewhereMapped` journal event is appended second, with no rollback if `append_event` fails. A journal write error will therefore leave a persisted mapping with no audit event.

Tighten stale-bead fallback so only definitive missing/closed bead results are downgraded to warnings. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:55-73`, any `br show` failure is treated as `bead_verified = false` and reconciliation proceeds. Timeouts, parse failures, or repo/configuration errors would therefore be misclassified as stale-bead cases instead of surfacing an operator-visible failure.

details.body: In `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments` (engine.rs:7538), the `mapped_bead_verified` field is hardcoded to `false` with a comment saying verification is deferred to `automation_runtime`. However, the `automation_runtime::planned_elsewhere::reconcile_planned_elsewhere` function — which does perform verification — is never called from the engine or from `success_reconciliation`. The engine records unverified mappings, but there is no code path that later invokes the verification handler to update them. Either the automation_runtime reconciler should be wired into the success_reconciliation flow to verify pending unverified mappings, or the engine should call `reconcile_planned_elsewhere` directly instead of `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments`. As it stands, `mapped_bead_verified` will always be `false` for mappings created through the engine path.

There are no tests for the `mapped_to_bead_id: Some(...)` path in the final_review aggregation logic. The `has_non_planned_elsewhere` flag, the partitioning of amendments into planned-elsewhere vs regular in the engine, and the no-restart behavior when all amendments are planned-elsewhere are all untested. The existing final_review tests only set `mapped_to_bead_id: None`. At minimum, add a test for `aggregate_final_review_panel` where some amendments have `mapped_to_bead_id: Some("...")` to verify that `restart_required` is false when all accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere.

The shipped execution path never performs the stale-bead lookup or optional `br comment` that this feature is supposed to add. The final-review engine records planned-elsewhere items by calling `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments` from `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2655-2668` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2864-2878`, and that helper hardcodes `mapped_bead_verified: false` before writing the mapping in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7607-7613`. The only code that actually verifies the mapped bead and posts comments lives in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:103-157`, but this branch does not route the production final-review flow through that handler. As a result, stale bead references are never detected in production and the optional `br comment` path is unreachable.

Planned-elsewhere mappings are persisted before the final-review stage commit is known to have succeeded, and they are not rolled back if the stage later fails. In the completion path, mappings are written at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2655-2668` before the stage-completed journal append at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2670-2698`. In the restart path, mappings are written at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2864-2878` before queue writes and later failure handling at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2880-2975`. Because `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` appends directly to the milestone journal and `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2353-2390`, any later stage failure leaves behind durable mapping state for a final-review attempt that never committed, and retries will append duplicates.

Final-review deduplication collapses amendments solely by normalized body and silently keeps the first non-`None` mapping target, so conflicting ownership classifications can be misrouted. `merge_final_review_amendments` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:154-183` uses only `normalized_body` as the dedup key and, on collision, preserves whatever `mapped_to_bead_id` it saw first. Later, restart behavior is decided purely from that surviving field in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1533-1591`. If two reviewers submit the same amendment text but disagree on whether it is planned-elsewhere, or disagree on which bead owns it, one reviewer's classification is discarded based on proposal order and the run can incorrectly skip a restart or record the mapping against the wrong bead.
